segments.text: third millennium BC
segments.translation: 公元前三千年，

segments.text: Mesopotamian Kings recorded and interpreted their dreams on wax tablets

segments.text: A Thousand Years Later ancient
segments.translation: 一千年之后，

segments.text: Egyptians wrote a dream book
segments.translation: 古埃及人写了一本关于梦的书

segments.text: listing over a hundred common dreams and their meanings
segments.translation: 列出了超过一百个日常的梦以及它们的解释。

segments.text: and in the years since
segments.translation: 多年之后，

segments.text: we haven't paused in our quest to understand why we dream so
segments.translation: 我们从未停止过探索为什么我们会做梦

segments.text: after a great deal of scientific research
segments.translation: 所以，在进行了大量的科学研究，

segments.translation: 经历了科技进步，

segments.text: persistence
segments.translation: 以及我们的坚持不懈之后，

segments.text: we still don't have any definite answers but we have some theories
segments.translation:
我们依旧没有任何确切的答案，
但是我们已经发展了一些有趣的理论。

segments.text: we dream to fulfill our wishes
segments.translation: 我们为了满足自己的愿望而做梦。

segments.text: in the early 1900s
segments.translation: 在19世纪早期，

segments.text: Sigman Freud proposed that while all of our dreams including our nightmares
segments.translation: 西格蒙德·佛洛依德提出，虽然我们所有的梦，包括梦魇，

segments.text: are a collection of images from our daily conscious lives
segments.translation: 都来自日常有意识生活的画面集合，

segments.text: they also have symbolic meanings
segments.translation: 他们也有一些抽象的意义，

segments.text: which relate to the Fulfillment of our subconscious wishes Freud
segments.translation: 和满足我们潜意识中的愿望有关。

segments.text: theorized that everything we remember when we wake up from a dream
segments.translation: 佛洛依德提出，我们早上从梦中醒来所记得的所有事情

segments.text: is a symbolic iic representation of our unconscious primitive thoughts urges and desires
segments.translation: 都是我们无意识中最原始的想法，冲动，以及欲望的

segments.text: Freud
segments.translation: 象征性代表

segments.text: believed that by analyzing those remembered elements
segments.translation: 弗洛伊德相信，通过分析这些我们记得的元素，

segments.text: the unconscious content would be revealed to our conscious mind and
segments.translation: 那些无意识的内容都可以反映出我们有意思的思想，

segments.text: psychological issues stemming from its repression
segments.translation: 以及精神上受到的压抑

segments.text: could be addressed and resolved
segments.translation: 都可以被理解和解答。

segments.translation: 我们做梦是为了记忆。

segments.text: increase performance on certain mental tasks
segments.translation: 为了加强一些脑力工作上的表现，

segments.text: sleep is good but dreaming
segments.translation: 睡眠是非常有效的，

segments.translation: 然而睡觉时做梦效果会更好。

segments.text: 2010 researchers found
segments.translation: 2010年，研究人员发现

segments.text: that subjects were much better at getting through a complex 3D maze if they had napped and dreamed
segments.translation: 如果被试者在第二次尝试穿越迷宫之前小憩一会儿并且做了一会儿梦的话，

segments.text: of the maze prior to their second attempt
segments.translation: 他们在穿越一个复杂的三维迷宫时会表现更佳。

segments.text: in fact they were up to 10 times better at it
segments.translation: 事实上，他们比那些在两次之间醒着思考迷宫的人，

segments.text: than those who only thought of the maze while awake between attempts
segments.translation: 以及那些虽然睡了但是没有做梦的人

segments.text: and those who napped but did not dream about the maze researchers
segments.translation: 表现要好十倍以上。

segments.text: theorize that certain memory processes

segments.translation: 如果没有做梦来产生这种

segments.translation: 我们为了预演而做梦。

segments.translation: 而原始本能理论认为

segments.text: but it doesn't always have to be unpleasant
